mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in industrial production (INDPRO) captures real output growth that translates into higher corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days through a persistent cash-flow channel.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC is below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of top-quartile INDPRO growth months.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses 1.0 percentage point above its 12-month trailing minimum, labor market distress triggers forced de-risking and earnings downgrades, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE exceeds its 12-month trailing minimum by 1.0pp or more
failure_condition: Negative 60-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered months, or the mean return in triggered months is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Joint month-over-month increases in the federal funds rate and consumer prices produce a stagflationary discount-rate shock that compresses equity multiples through rising real yields, an effect absent when only one series rises, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises month-over-month and CPIAUCSL rises month-over-month
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in fewer than 60% of joint-increase months, or the interaction term in a bivariate regression has a positive coefficient (p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recession periods, month-over-month growth in retail sales (RSAFS) signals resilient household consumption that limits corporate revenue downside, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days; this protective effect vanishes outside of crisis regimes.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns follow fewer than 55% of positive RSAFS surprises during post-shock recession months, or the mechanism generates negative mean returns in that regime.

mechanism_narrative: Three consecutive monthly declines in housing starts (HOUST) signal deepening contraction in construction activity and durable goods demand, triggering negative earnings revisions and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 60-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered 3-month decline sequences, or the mean excess return in triggered periods is positive.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflect improving labor market conditions that boost household income expectations and consumption, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days through a high-frequency sentiment channel.
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS change and 5-day forward return is below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter declines in real GDP (GDPC1) signal broad economic contraction that reduces corporate profit growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 60-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of quarters with negative GDPC1 growth, or the Spearman correlation between GDPC1 change and 60-day return is positive.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 5.0% tightens financial conditions sufficiently to raise default risk premia and depress equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of months with FEDFUNDS above 5.0%, or mean return in those months is not significantly negative.
